definition: The equity risk premium is regime-inconsistent: under positive correlation assumptions, the S&P 500 is 12-18% overvalued (fair value ~4,650-4,900 vs. ~5,500-5,700), while roughly fairly valued under negative correlation assumptions. Three-method decomposition reveals mega-cap implied ERP of ~2.0-3.0% vs. S&P 493 at ~5.0-6.5%.
evidence: Fed model spread at ~+30bp, lowest since 2007
Cross-sectional ERP decomposition labeled 'single most actionable insight' in pair_2 debate
Under positive correlation, required ERP rises 50-100bp
open_questions: Entirely conditional on which correlation regime prevails
Fed model component critiqued for comparing real to nominal quantity (Asness 2003)
How should capital allocation shift based on conditional ERP?
